info.title: resend-service API
info.description: Receives signed Resend webhooks and provides bearer-authenticated, topic-centered email conversations. The service preserves RFC reply relationships, supports synchronous and transactional-outbox sends, and stores untrusted email content without retrieving attachments.

tags.name: Conversations
tags.description: Create, assign, and hydrate email conversations

tags.name: Messages
tags.description: Send messages in an existing conversation

tags.name: Outbox
tags.description: Asynchronous message enqueue and bounded batch delivery

get.description: Checks configuration for every webhook, conversation, and outbox capability plus PostgreSQL connectivity without returning sensitive details.

503.description: Required configuration or PostgreSQL is unavailable.

post.description: Verifies the Svix signature against the exact raw request body, classifies the event, and stores its normalized fields. For email.received, the service retrieves the text, HTML, and headers from Resend and updates the conversation projection; attachments are not fetched. Eligible RFC Message-ID ancestry remains authoritative. When RFC ancestry does not resolve a conversation, a recognized conversation token in to or received_for is used only when the sender matches that conversation's participant. All three Svix headers are required and must describe the submitted body. A delivery using an svix-id already stored for the same event family is acknowledged with the same 200 response after projection succeeds. Resend may retry non-200 responses. Swagger UI cannot generate valid Svix signatures; interactive requests require header values generated for the exact raw request body.

requestBody.description: A supported Resend event serialized as JSON. The serialized bytes must be the same bytes used to generate svix-signature.

200.description: The event was stored, or an event with the same svix-id was already stored for its event family.

400.description: One or more required Svix headers are missing, or the signed event type is unsupported.

401.description: The Svix signature could not be verified against the exact raw request body.

500.description: The server is missing required configuration, could not retrieve inbound content, or could not persist the event and projection. Resend can retry the delivery.

post.description: Creates the single conversation for an external topic and sends its opening email. The Idempotency-Key is retained indefinitely by this service, while provider requests use an internal provider-scoped idempotency value. Repeating the same key and normalized request returns the existing message; reusing the key with a different normalized request returns 409. Subject values are normalized by stripping leading Re:/Fw:/Fwd: prefixes case-insensitively.

200.description: An earlier completed request with this idempotency key was returned.

201.description: Conversation created (or a failed topic conversation re-opened with a new idempotency key) and the opening email accepted by Resend.

202.description: An earlier request with this idempotency key is still pending.

409.description: The idempotency key was used for a different request, or the topic already owns a conversation with at least one message that is not failed. A topic conversation whose messages have all failed is re-opened instead: a new idempotency key creates and sends a fresh opening message.

502.description: Resend did not accept the email or the send result is indeterminate. The response includes the persisted conversation and message identifiers.

get.description: Returns inbound conversations whose RFC headers did not match a topic-backed conversation.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by the previous unassigned-conversation page.

before.description: Opaque base64url cursor for the next unassigned-conversation page.

post.description: Atomically creates the conversation, pending opening message, and outbox entry without sending to Resend. The request body matches the synchronous conversation-start operation. A topic whose conversation messages have all failed may be reopened with a new idempotency key.

200.description: An earlier completed outbox request with this idempotency key was returned.

202.description: The persisted opening message is pending outbox delivery.

502.description: An earlier outbox request completed as failed or indeterminate.

post.description: Claims or reclaims one persistent batch and makes at most one bounded Resend batch request. Transient failures retain exact ordered membership for a later idempotent retry.

200.description: The bounded drain invocation completed, including persisted retry scheduling when applicable.

get.summary: Get a conversation by service ID

200.description: Conversation and a chronological page of messages.

patch.description: Assigns a topic exactly once. Assignment fails if the conversation already has a topic or the topic already belongs to another conversation.

post.description: Sends a reply to an explicit parent message or, when omitted, the latest accepted or received message. In-Reply-To is the parent RFC Message-ID. References is the parent's ancestry followed by that parent identifier.

200.description: An earlier completed request with this idempotency key was returned.

202.description: An earlier request with this idempotency key is still pending.

502.description: Resend did not accept the email or the send result is indeterminate.

value.error: Email was not confirmed as sent

503.description: The selected parent RFC Message-ID could not be retrieved.

example.error: Reply parent threading metadata is unavailable

post.description: Resolves and freezes the reply parent and RFC ancestry, then atomically creates a pending message and outbox entry without sending it. The request body matches the synchronous reply operation.

200.description: An earlier completed outbox request with this idempotency key was returned.

202.description: The persisted reply is pending outbox delivery.

502.description: An earlier outbox request completed as failed or indeterminate.

503.description: The selected parent RFC Message-ID could not be retrieved.

get.summary: Get a conversation by external topic

200.description: Conversation and a chronological page of messages.

bearerAuth.description: Service credential shared only with authorized private-network callers.

outboxDrainAuth.description: Dedicated credential for the private scheduled outbox drain caller.

svixId.name: svix-id
svixId.description: Unique Svix message identifier used for signature verification and idempotency.

svixTimestamp.name: svix-timestamp
svixTimestamp.description: Svix delivery timestamp included in signature verification.

svixSignature.name: svix-signature
svixSignature.description: Svix signature generated for the exact raw request body.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Caller-generated key unique to one logical send request. Idempotency compares normalized validated fields; unknown body properties are ignored and do not produce a conflict. Keys are globally unique across synchronous and outbox delivery modes.

Before.name: before
Before.description: Return messages older than this internal message ID.

title.description: Trimmed, header-safe text: at least one non-whitespace character and no ASCII control characters.

email.description: Runtime validation also limits the address to 320 characters.

name.description: Header-safe text: no ASCII control characters. Blank values are normalized to null.

text.description: Plain text body. Must be a nonempty string when present.

html.description: HTML body. Must be a nonempty string when present.

replyToName.description: Optional per-message display name for the service-generated Reply-To address. Blank or null values are omitted. The generated address is unchanged.

subject.description: Defaults to the topic title after trimming and stripping leading Re:/Fw:/Fwd: prefixes. Header-safe text: no ASCII control characters. Blank values are omitted.

replyToMessageId.description: Internal message ID. Defaults to the latest accepted or received message.

limit.description: Maximum number of unbatched messages to assign when creating a new batch.

stateDetail.description: Human-readable provider or runtime detail. Not stable for machine parsing.

deliveryState.description: Outbound-only delivery lifecycle projected from Resend delivery webhooks. Inbound messages return null. unknown means no delivery lifecycle webhook has been projected yet.

deliveryStateDetail.description: Human-readable delivery detail from Resend webhook payloads. Not stable for machine parsing.

deliveredAt.description: Timestamp from the first projected email.delivered webhook for outbound messages, or null when no delivery confirmation has been projected.

replyTo.description: For outbound messages, the service-generated conversation routing address. For inbound messages, the sender-provided Reply-To address when present.

replyToName.description: For outbound messages, the optional per-message display name used with the service-generated Reply-To address. Inbound messages currently return null.

replyToAddress.description: Stable service-generated Reply-To address used for this conversation's outbound messages.

from.description: Sender address as supplied by Resend.

message_id.description: RFC Message-ID supplied for inbound email.received events.

reason.description: Provider-supplied failure reason, such as reached_daily_quota.

BadRequest.description: The request is malformed or failed validation.

Unauthorized.description: The bearer credential is missing or invalid. Bearer-protected endpoints include `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer`.

WWW-Authenticate.description: Bearer authentication challenge returned by bearer-protected endpoints.

NotFound.description: The requested conversation or message does not exist.

Conflict.description: The topic, assignment, or idempotency key conflicts with existing state.

ServerError.description: The service is misconfigured or could not persist the operation.
